name: 17p12_PMP22_CMT1A_HNPP
description: 17p12 CMT1A region — a 1.4 Mb interval flanked by two ~24 kb directly oriented CMT1A-REPs at >99.7% identity. NAHR between the CMT1A-REPs produces the reciprocal CMT1A duplication (Charcot-Marie-Tooth type 1A) and HNPP deletion (Hereditary Neuropathy with liability to Pressure Palsies); PMP22 is the dosage-sensitive gene. Archetypal example of NAHR-mediated genomic disorder (Lupski 1991).

architecture.internal_structure: Proximal and distal CMT1A-REPs of ~24 kb at >99.7% identity flanking PMP22, COX10, HS3ST3B1, TEKT3. A 1.5 kb hotspot within the REPs accounts for >75% of NAHR events.

related_diseases: Hereditary Neuropathy with liability to Pressure Palsies (HNPP deletion)
